ex1: A black robotic arm is shown in a clean, modern indoor setting, positioned behind a dark green rubber mat placed on a white tabletop. The base of the robotic arm is fixed to the tabletop. On the mat, a small white polyhedral object is positioned at the start location on the right side, while the goal region is marked by the smaller white circle on the left side. Two white horizontal lines define a straight corridor between the start and goal regions. The black robot gripper moves towards the white object and pushes it from right to left along the corridor toward the goal. When the white object is to the left of the goal circle, the black gripper always moves above the white object and touches it from the left. Throughout the task, the white object remains within the corridor. The white object always moves in a straight line and the black gripper is always close to the white object. Each time after the white object comes to rest, the black gripper responds by making controlled corrective adjustments, guiding the object back onto the intended path and continuing to push it toward the goal circle. The final frame shows the white polyhedral object inside the left goal circle.
ex2: A black robotic arm is shown in a clean, modern indoor setting, positioned behind a dark green rubber mat placed on a white tabletop. The base of the robotic arm is fixed to the tabletop. On the mat, a small white polyhedral object is positioned at the start location on the right side, while the goal region is marked by a smaller white circle on the left side. A red obstacle is placed near the midpoint of the direct path between the start and goal regions. The black robot gripper contacts the white object and pushes it from right to left toward the goal circle. The white object follows an indirect path around the obstacle instead of moving in a straight line. Throughout the task, neither the white object nor the black gripper touches or moves the obstacle. The red obstacle remains stationary throughout the sequence. The white object always moves in a straight line and the black gripper is always close to the white object. Each time after the white object comes to rest, the black gripper makes controlled corrective adjustments to guide it around the obstacle and toward the goal. The final frame shows the white polyhedral object inside the left goal circle, while the obstacle remains in its original position.
